target.name: Monarch Quantum

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $115M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Monarch Quantum is a Growth-stage quantum computing company founded by Dr. Timothy Day, a Stanford-trained physicist and co-founder of Daylight Solutions (acquired by Leonardo DRS in 2017). The company has raised $115M+ in capital and contracts. The quantum computing sector comprises 3 public comparables (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 7 private peers including Quantinuum and Infleqtion, though the narrow peer set limits direct valuation anchoring.

Given the scarcity of Growth-stage quantum computing peers with sufficient comparable data, only stage-progression analysis is applicable here. Using the $115M total raised as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a standard 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-to-next-stage transitions yields an implied valuation of $138M. This method does not rely on peer multiples but rather on capital deployment and typical progression mechanics.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $117M to $159M, centered on $138M. This range reflects conservative to optimistic interpretations of the stage-progression method, with the low end representing a tighter read and the high end assuming stronger momentum. The primary uncertainty is the absence of sufficient narrow-set comparables—7 private peers exist in quantum computing but none meet simultaneous filters for stage, scale, and capital efficiency. A full peer-based triangulation remains infeasible until either more Growth-stage quantum companies reach demonstrable revenue milestones or Monarch's own financial metrics become available for ratio analysis.
